what: Feature Selection via Correlation or Differential Expression — feature_select
title: Feature Selection via Correlation or Differential Expression — feature_select
text: Selects informative features using either correlation quantitative response differential expression (limma) binary/continuous responses.

title: Feature Selection via Correlation or Differential Expression — feature_select
code: feature_select(   x,   y,   method = c("cor", "dif"),   family = c("spearman", "pearson"),   cutoff = NULL,   padjcut = NULL )

title: Feature Selection via Correlation or Differential Expression — feature_select
text: x Numeric matrix. Features (rows) samples (columns). y Numeric factor. Response vector (quantitative binary). method Character. "cor" (correlation) "dif" (differential expression). Default c("cor","dif"). family Character. Correlation method method = "cor": "spearman" "pearson". cutoff Numeric. Absolute correlation (cor) |log2FC| (dif) threshold. padjcut Numeric. Adjusted p-value cutoff.

title: Feature Selection via Correlation or Differential Expression — feature_select
text: Character vector selected feature names.

title: Feature Selection via Correlation or Differential Expression — feature_select
code: # Simulate data set.seed(123) sim_eset <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 50), 100, 50) rownames(sim_eset) <- c("PDCD1", paste0("Gene", 2:100)) colnames(sim_eset) <- paste0("Sample", 1:50) pd1 <- as.numeric(sim_eset["PDCD1", ]) group <- ifelse(pd1 > mean(pd1), "high", "low")  # Correlation method pd1_cor <- feature_select(   x = sim_eset, y = pd1, method = "cor",   family = "pearson", padjcut = 0.05, cutoff = 0.5 ) #> Deteching more than two levels in y, we will treat y as a quantitative varibale  # Differential expression method pd1_dif <- feature_select(   x = sim_eset, y = pd1, method = "dif",   padjcut = 0.05, cutoff = 2 ) #> Deteching more than two levels in y, we will treat y as a quantitative varibale #> For quantitative varibale, upper 25% and bottom 25% samples #>               were treated as upregulated group and downregulated group. pd1_dif_2 <- feature_select(   x = sim_eset, y = group,   method = "dif", padjcut = 0.05, cutoff = 2 ) #> Deteching two levels in y, we will treat y as a binary varibale

what: Analyze Mutations Related to Signature Scores — find_mutations
title: Analyze Mutations Related to Signature Scores — find_mutations
text: function identifies mutations associated specific signature score, performs statistical tests significance, generates oncoprints box plots visualize relationships.

title: Analyze Mutations Related to Signature Scores — find_mutations
code: find_mutations(   mutation_matrix,   signature_matrix,   id_signature_matrix = "ID",   signature,   min_mut_freq = 0.05,   plot = TRUE,   method = "multi",   point_alpha = 0.1,   save_path = NULL,   palette = "jco",   cols = NULL,   show_plot = TRUE,   show_col = FALSE,   width = 8,   height = 4,   oncoprint_group_by = "mean",   oncoprint_col = "#224444",   gene_counts = 10,   jitter = FALSE,   genes = NULL,   point_size = 4.5 )

title: Analyze Mutations Related to Signature Scores — find_mutations
text: mutation_matrix matrix mutation data samples rows genes columns. signature_matrix data frame sample identifiers signature scores. id_signature_matrix Column name `signature_matrix` sample identifiers. signature Name target signature analysis. min_mut_freq Minimum mutation frequency required gene inclusion. Default 0.05. plot Logical indicating whether generate save plots. Default TRUE. method Statistical test method: "multi" Cuzick Wilcoxon, "Wilcoxon" . Default "multi". point_alpha Transparency points box plot. Default 0.1. save_path Directory save plots results. NULL, files saved. palette Color palette box plots(used cols NULL). Default "jco". cols Character vector. Custom colors box plots. NULL, uses palette. Default NULL. show_plot Logical indicating whether display plots. Default TRUE. show_col Logical indicating whether show color codes. Default FALSE. width Width oncoprint plot. Default 8. height Height oncoprint plot. Default 4. oncoprint_group_by Grouping method oncoprint: "mean" "quantile". Default "mean". oncoprint_col Color mutations oncoprint. Default "#224444". gene_counts Number genes display oncoprint. Default 10. jitter Logical indicating whether add jitter box plot points. Default FALSE. genes Optional vector gene names; NULL, selects based frequency. point_size Size points box plot. Default 4.5.

title: Analyze Mutations Related to Signature Scores — find_mutations
text: list containing statistical test results, oncoprint plots, box plots.

title: Analyze Mutations Related to Signature Scores — find_mutations

title: Generate Reference Signature Matrix — generateRef
text: Generates reference signature matrix cell types based differential expression analysis. Supports limma normalized data DESeq2 raw count data.

title: Generate Reference Signature Matrix — generateRef
code: generateRef(dds, pheno, FDR = 0.05, dat, method = "limma")

title: Generate Reference Signature Matrix — generateRef
text: dds Matrix. Raw count data RNA-seq. Required `method = "DESeq2"`. pheno Character vector. Cell type class samples. FDR Numeric. Genes BH adjusted p-value < FDR considered significant. Default 0.05. dat Matrix data frame. Normalized transcript quantification data (e.g., FPKM, TPM). method Character. Method differential expression: `"limma"` `"DESeq2"`. Default `"limma"`.

title: Generate Reference Signature Matrix — generateRef
text: List containing:   - `reference_matrix`: Data frame median expression significant     genes across cell types.   - `G`: Optimal number probes minimizing condition number.   - `condition_number`: Minimum condition number.   - `whole_matrix`: Full median expression matrix.

title: Generate Reference Signature Matrix — generateRef
code: expressionData <- matrix(runif(1000 * 4, min = 0, max = 10), ncol = 4) rownames(expressionData) <- paste("Gene", 1:1000, sep = "_") colnames(expressionData) <- paste("Sample", 1:4, sep = "_")  phenotype <- c("celltype1", "celltype2", "celltype1", "celltype2")  rawCountData <- matrix(sample(1:100, 1000 * 4, replace = TRUE), ncol = 4) rownames(rawCountData) <- paste("Gene", 1:1000, sep = "_") colnames(rawCountData) <- paste("Sample", 1:4, sep = "_")  # \donttest{ result <- generateRef(   dds = rawCountData, pheno = phenotype,   FDR = 0.05, dat = expressionData, method = "DESeq2" ) #> ℹ Running differentially expressed genes using DESeq2 #> converting counts to integer mode #> Warning: some variables in design formula are characters, converting to factors #> using pre-existing size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing #> converting counts to integer mode #> Warning: some variables in design formula are characters, converting to factors #> using pre-existing size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing # }

title: Generate Reference Signature Matrix Using DESeq2 — generateRef_DEseq2
code: generateRef_DEseq2(dds, pheno, FDR = 0.05, dat)

title: Generate Reference Signature Matrix Using DESeq2 — generateRef_DEseq2
text: dds Matrix. Raw count data RNA-seq. pheno Character vector. Cell type classes samples. FDR Numeric. Threshold adjusted p-values. Default 0.05. dat Matrix. Normalized expression data (e.g., FPKM, TPM) calculating median expression.

title: Generate Reference Signature Matrix Using DESeq2 — generateRef_DEseq2
text: List containing:   - `reference_matrix`: Data frame median expression significant     genes across cell types.   - `G`: Optimal number probes minimizing condition number.   - `condition_number`: Minimum condition number.   - `whole_matrix`: Full median expression matrix.

title: Generate Reference Signature Matrix Using DESeq2 — generateRef_DEseq2
code: set.seed(123) dds <- matrix(sample(0:1000, 2000, replace = TRUE), nrow = 100, ncol = 20) colnames(dds) <- paste("Sample", 1:20, sep = "_") rownames(dds) <- paste("Gene", 1:100, sep = "_") pheno <- rep(c("Type1", "Type2"), each = 10) dat <- matrix(runif(2000), nrow = 100, ncol = 20) rownames(dat) <- rownames(dds) colnames(dat) <- colnames(dds) # \donttest{ result <- generateRef_DEseq2(dds = dds, pheno = pheno, FDR = 0.05, dat = dat) #> converting counts to integer mode #> Warning: some variables in design formula are characters, converting to factors #> using pre-existing size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing #> converting counts to integer mode #> Warning: some variables in design formula are characters, converting to factors #> using pre-existing size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing print(result$reference_matrix) #> [1] Type1 Type2 #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names) # }

code: dds <- matrix(rpois(200 * 10, lambda = 10), ncol = 10) rownames(dds) <- paste("Gene", 1:200, sep = "_") colnames(dds) <- paste("Sample", 1:10, sep = "_") pheno <- sample(c("Type1", "Type2", "Type3"), 10, replace = TRUE) dat <- matrix(rnorm(200 * 10), ncol = 10) rownames(dat) <- rownames(dds) colnames(dat) <- colnames(dds) # \donttest{ results <- generateRef_rnaseq(dds = dds, pheno = pheno, FDR = 0.05, dat = dat) #> converting counts to integer mode #> Warning: some variables in design formula are characters, converting to factors #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing #> converting counts to integer mode #> Warning: some variables in design formula are characters, converting to factors #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing #> converting counts to integer mode #> Warning: some variables in design formula are characters, converting to factors #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing # }

title: Extract Hazard Ratio and Confidence Intervals from Cox Model — getHRandCIfromCoxph
text: Extracts hazard ratio (HR) 95 Cox proportional hazards model.

code: getHRandCIfromCoxph(coxphData)

title: Extract Hazard Ratio and Confidence Intervals from Cox Model — getHRandCIfromCoxph
text: coxphData Fitted Cox model object `survival::coxph()`.

title: Extract Hazard Ratio and Confidence Intervals from Cox Model — getHRandCIfromCoxph
text: Data frame p-values, HR, confidence intervals.

title: Extract Hazard Ratio and Confidence Intervals from Cox Model — getHRandCIfromCoxph
text: Dorothee Nickles Dongqiang Zeng

title: Extract Hazard Ratio and Confidence Intervals from Cox Model — getHRandCIfromCoxph
code: library(survival) set.seed(123) df <- data.frame(   TTE = rexp(200, rate = 0.1),   Cens = rbinom(200, size = 1, prob = 0.7),   group = sample(c("Treatment", "Control"), 200, replace = TRUE) ) coxphData <- survival::coxph(survival::Surv(TTE, Cens) ~ group, data = df) results <- getHRandCIfromCoxph(coxphData) print(results) #>                       P    HR CI_low_0.95 CI_up_0.95 #> groupTreatment 0.400853 0.868      0.6238     1.2077

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Between Two Variables — get_cor
code: get_cor(   eset,   pdata = NULL,   var1,   var2,   is.matrix = FALSE,   id_eset = "ID",   id_pdata = "ID",   scale = TRUE,   subtype = NULL,   na.subtype.rm = FALSE,   color_subtype = NULL,   palette = "jama",   index = NULL,   method = c("spearman", "pearson", "kendall"),   show_cor_result = TRUE,   col_line = NULL,   id = NULL,   show_label = FALSE,   point_size = 4,   title = NULL,   alpha = 0.5,   title_size = 1.5,   text_size = 10,   axis_angle = 0,   hjust = 0,   show_plot = TRUE,   save_plot = FALSE,   path = NULL,   fig.format = "png",   fig.width = 7,   fig.height = 7.3,   add.hdr.line = FALSE )

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Between Two Variables — get_cor
text: eset Dataset containing variables (data frame matrix). pdata Optional phenotype data frame. Default `NULL`. var1 Name first variable. var2 Name second variable. .matrix Logical indicating `eset` matrix features rows. Default `FALSE`. id_eset ID column `eset`. Default `"ID"`. id_pdata ID column `pdata`. Default `"ID"`. scale Logical indicating whether scale data. Default `TRUE`. subtype Optional grouping variable coloring points. Default `NULL`. na.subtype.rm Logical indicating whether remove NA subtype. Default `FALSE`. color_subtype Colors subtypes. Default `NULL`. palette Color palette name. Default `"jama"`. index Plot index filename. Default `NULL` (uses 1). method Correlation method: `"spearman"`, `"pearson"`, `"kendall"`. Default `"spearman"`. show_cor_result Logical indicating whether print correlation result. Default `TRUE`. col_line Color regression line. Default `NULL` (auto-determine). id Column point labels. Default `NULL`. show_label Logical indicating whether show labels. Default `FALSE`. point_size Size points. Default 4. title Plot title. Default `NULL`. alpha Transparency points. Default 0.5. title_size Title font size. Default 1.5. text_size Text font size. Default 10. axis_angle Axis label angle. Default 0. hjust Horizontal justification. Default 0. show_plot Logical indicating whether display plot. Default `TRUE`. save_plot Logical indicating whether save plot. Default `FALSE`. path Save path. Default `NULL`. fig.format Figure format: `"png"` `"pdf"`. Default `"png"`. fig.width Figure width inches. Default 7. fig.height Figure height inches. Default 7.3. add.hdr.line Logical adding HDR (high density region) lines. Default `FALSE`.

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Between Two Variables — get_cor
text: ggplot object correlation plot.

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Between Two Variables — get_cor
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Between Two Variables — get_cor
code: # Simulate data set.seed(123) sim_eset <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 20), 100, 20) rownames(sim_eset) <- c("GZMB", "CD274", paste0("Gene", 3:100)) colnames(sim_eset) <- paste0("Sample", 1:20)  # Calculate and plot correlation p <- get_cor(eset = sim_eset, is.matrix = TRUE, var1 = "GZMB", var2 = "CD274", show_plot = FALSE) #> ℹ Calculating spearman correlation (n = 20) #>  #> 	Spearman's rank correlation rho #>  #> data:  data[[var1]] and data[[var2]] #> S = 782, p-value = 0.07226 #> alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0 #> sample estimates: #>       rho  #> 0.4120301  #>  #> ℹ Exact p-value: 7.1e-02 if (!is.null(p)) print(p) #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

what: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
text: Calculates visualizes correlation matrix two sets variables. Supports Pearson, Spearman, Kendall correlation methods. function generates customizable heatmap significance stars.

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
code: get_cor_matrix(   data,   feas1,   feas2,   method = c("pearson", "spearman", "kendall"),   path = NULL,   index = 1,   fig.type = "pdf",   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   project = NULL,   is.matrix = FALSE,   scale = TRUE,   font.size = 15,   fill_by_cor = FALSE,   round.num = 1,   font.size.star = 8,   cols = NULL )

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
text: data Input data frame matrix. Variables columns. feas1 Character vector variable names first set. feas2 Character vector variable names second set. method Correlation method: `"pearson"`, `"spearman"`, `"kendall"`. Default `"pearson"`. path Directory save plot. `NULL`, plot saved. Default `NULL`. index Numeric prefix output filename. Default 1. fig.type File format: `"pdf"`, `"png"`, etc. Default `"pdf"`. width Plot width inches. Auto-calculated `NULL`. height Plot height inches. Auto-calculated `NULL`. project Project name plot title. Default `NULL`. .matrix Logical: `TRUE`, data transposed. Default `FALSE`. scale Logical: scale variables correlation. Default `TRUE`. font.size Font size axis labels. Default 15. fill_by_cor Logical: show correlation values instead stars. Default `FALSE`. round.num Decimal places correlation values. Default 1. font.size.star Font size significance stars. Default 8. cols Custom colors gradient (low, mid, high). `NULL`, uses blue-white-red. Default `NULL`.

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
text: ggplot object displaying correlation matrix heatmap.

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Calculate and Visualize Correlation Matrix Between Two Variable Sets — get_cor_matrix
code: set.seed(123) data <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(1000), nrow = 100, ncol = 10)) colnames(data) <- paste0("Gene_", 1:10)  feas1 <- c("Gene_1", "Gene_2", "Gene_3") feas2 <- c("Gene_4", "Gene_5", "Gene_6")  cor_plot <- get_cor_matrix(   data = data,   feas1 = feas1,   feas2 = feas2,   method = "spearman",   project = "Example Correlation" ) #> ℹ Calculating spearman correlation: 3 x 3

title: Get IOBR Cache Directory — get_iobr_cache_dir
text: Returns current cache directory IOBR downloaded data. comply CRAN policies, default cache directory session-specific temporary directory. Users can opt-persistent cache setting `options(IOBR.cache_dir = "/path")` using `set_iobr_cache_dir()`. cache directory determined following priority order: 1. Function argument `cache_dir` (provided) 2. Option `IOBR.cache_dir` (set via `options()`) 3. Default: session-specific temporary directory (`file.path(tempdir(), "IOBR_cache")`)

what: Integrative Correlation Analysis Between Phenotype and Features — iobr_cor_plot

title: Integrative Correlation Analysis Between Phenotype and Features — iobr_cor_plot
code: iobr_cor_plot(   pdata_group,   id1 = "ID",   feature_data,   id2 = "ID",   target = NULL,   group = "group3",   is_target_continuous = TRUE,   padj_cutoff = 1,   index = 1,   category = "signature",   signature_group = NULL,   ProjectID = "TCGA",   palette_box = "nrc",   cols_box = NULL,   palette_corplot = "pheatmap",   palette_heatmap = 2,   feature_limit = 26,   character_limit = 60,   show_heatmap_col_name = FALSE,   show_col = FALSE,   show_plot = FALSE,   path = NULL,   discrete_x = 20,   discrete_width = 20,   show_palettes = FALSE,   fig.type = "pdf" )

title: Integrative Correlation Analysis Between Phenotype and Features — iobr_cor_plot
text: pdata_group Data frame containing phenotype data identifier column. id1 Character string specifying column name `pdata_group` serving sample identifier. Default `"ID"`. feature_data Data frame containing feature data corresponding identifiers. id2 Character string specifying column name `feature_data` serving sample identifier. Default `"ID"`. target Character string specifying target variable column name continuous analysis. Default `NULL`. group Character string specifying grouping variable name categorical analysis. Default `"group3"`. is_target_continuous Logical indicating whether target variable continuous, affects grouping strategy. Default `TRUE`. padj_cutoff Numeric value specifying adjusted p-value cutoff filtering features. Default `1`. index Numeric index used ordering output file names. Default `1`. category Character string specifying data category: `"signature"` `"gene"`. signature_group List specifying grouping variable signatures. Options include `"sig_group"` signature grouping `"signature_collection"`/`"signature_tme"` gene grouping. ProjectID Character string specifying project identifier file naming. palette_box Character string integer specifying color palette box plots. Default `"nrc"`. cols_box Character vector specific colors box plots. Default `NULL`. palette_corplot Character string integer specifying color palette correlation plots. Default `"pheatmap"`. palette_heatmap Integer specifying color palette index heatmaps. Default `2`. feature_limit Integer specifying maximum number features display. Default `26`. character_limit Integer specifying maximum number characters variable labels. Default `60`. show_heatmap_col_name Logical indicating whether display column names heatmaps. Default `FALSE`. show_col Logical indicating whether display color codes palettes. Default `FALSE`. show_plot Logical indicating whether display plots. Default `FALSE`. path Character string specifying directory path saving output files. Default `NULL`. discrete_x Numeric threshold character length beyond labels discretized. Default `20`. discrete_width Numeric value specifying width label wrapping plots. Default `20`. show_palettes Logical indicating whether display color palettes. Default `FALSE`. fig.type Character string specifying format saving figures (`"pdf"`, `"png"`, etc.). Default `"pdf"`.

title: Integrative Correlation Analysis Between Phenotype and Features — iobr_cor_plot
text: Depending configuration, returns ggplot2 objects (box plots, heatmaps,   correlation plots) /data frame containing statistical analysis results.

title: Integrative Correlation Analysis Between Phenotype and Features — iobr_cor_plot
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Integrative Correlation Analysis Between Phenotype and Features — iobr_cor_plot
code: set.seed(123)  pdata_group <- data.frame(   ID = 1:100,   phenotype_score = rnorm(100) )  feature_data <- data.frame(   ID = 1:100,   Feature1 = rnorm(100),   Feature2 = rnorm(100),   Feature3 = rnorm(100) )  sig_group_example <- list(   signature = c("Feature1", "Feature2", "Feature3") )  results <- iobr_cor_plot(   pdata_group = pdata_group,   feature_data = feature_data,   id1 = "ID",   id2 = "ID",   target = "phenotype_score",   is_target_continuous = TRUE,   category = "signature",   signature_group = sig_group_example,   show_plot = FALSE,   path = tempdir() ) #> ℹ Processing signature: "signature" #> tidyHeatmap says: (once per session) from release 1.7.0 the scaling is set to "none" by default. Please use scale = "row", "column" or "both" to apply scaling #> tidyHeatmap says: If you use tidyHeatmap for scientific research, please cite: Mangiola, S. and Papenfuss, A.T., 2020. 'tidyHeatmap: an R package for modular heatmap production based on tidy principles.' Journal of Open Source Software. doi:10.21105/joss.02472. #> This message is displayed once per session. #> Warning: `when()` was deprecated in purrr 1.0.0. #> ℹ Please use `if` instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the tidyHeatmap package. #>   Please report the issue at #>   <https://github.com/stemangiola/tidyHeatmap/issues>. #> ℹ Heatmap3 palettes: pheatmap, peach, blues, virids, reds, normal  #> ℹ Computing spearman correlation for 3 features #> ✔ Correlation analysis complete  print(results) #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   sig_names p.value statistic p.adj log10pvalue stars #>   <chr>       <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>       <dbl> <fct> #> 1 Feature2    0.225  -0.122   0.493      0.647  "+"   #> 2 Feature3    0.328  -0.0987  0.493      0.484  "+"   #> 3 Feature1    0.969   0.00390 0.969      0.0136 ""

title: Tumor Microenvironment (TME) Deconvolution Pipeline — iobr_deconvo_pipeline
text: Executes integrated TME analysis gene expression matrix: performs immune/stromal cell deconvolution using multiple algorithms, computes signature scores, aggregates results. Designed exploratory immunogenomic profiling.

title: Tumor Microenvironment (TME) Deconvolution Pipeline — iobr_deconvo_pipeline
code: iobr_deconvo_pipeline(   eset,   project,   array,   tumor_type,   path = NULL,   permutation = 1000 )

title: Tumor Microenvironment (TME) Deconvolution Pipeline — iobr_deconvo_pipeline
text: eset Numeric matrix. Gene expression (TPM/log scale) genes rows. project Character. Project name (used output naming). array Logical. Whether data originated array platform. Affects deconvolution choices. tumor_type Character. Tumor type code (e.g., "stad") used certain methods. path Character. Output directory. Default NULL (uses tempdir()). permutation Integer. Number permutations CIBERSORT (similar). Default 1000.

title: Tumor Microenvironment (TME) Deconvolution Pipeline — iobr_deconvo_pipeline
text: Data frame integrating cell fractions signature scores (also writes intermediate outputs disk).

title: Tumor Microenvironment (TME) Deconvolution Pipeline — iobr_deconvo_pipeline
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Tumor Microenvironment (TME) Deconvolution Pipeline — iobr_deconvo_pipeline
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lm22 <- load_data("lm22") cancer_genes <- load_data("cancer_type_genes") if (!is.null(lm22) && !is.null(cancer_genes)) {   set.seed(123)   genes <- rownames(lm22)   xcell <- load_data("xCell.data")   if (!is.null(xcell)) genes <- unique(c(genes, xcell$genes))   genes <- unique(c(genes, cancer_genes[["stad"]]))   eset <- matrix(runif(length(genes) * 2), nrow = length(genes), ncol = 2)   rownames(eset) <- genes   colnames(eset) <- paste0("Sample", 1:2)   res <- iobr_deconvo_pipeline(     eset = eset, project = "TEST",     array = FALSE, tumor_type = "stad",     path = tempdir(), permutation = 2   )   if (!is.null(res)) head(res) } } # }

title: Differential Expression Analysis — iobr_deg
text: Performs differential expression analysis gene expression data using either DESeq2 limma. Includes pre-processing steps like filtering low count data, calculates fold changes adjusted p-values. Optionally generates volcano plots heatmaps.

title: Differential Expression Analysis — iobr_deg
code: iobr_deg(   eset,   annotation = NULL,   id_anno = NULL,   pdata,   group_id = "group",   pdata_id = "ID",   array = FALSE,   method = c("DESeq2", "limma"),   contrast = c("High", "Low"),   path = NULL,   padj_cutoff = 0.01,   logfc_cutoff = 0.5,   volcano_plot = FALSE,   col_volcano = 1,   heatmap = TRUE,   col_heatmap = 1,   parallel = FALSE )

title: Differential Expression Analysis — iobr_deg
text: eset matrix gene expression data rows represent genes columns represent samples. annotation Optional data frame mapping gene IDs gene names. Default `NULL`. id_anno Character string specifying identifier column annotation. Default `NULL`. pdata data frame containing sample information grouping labels. group_id Character string specifying column name `pdata` containing grouping labels. Default `"group"`. pdata_id Character string specifying column name `pdata` sample IDs. Default `"ID"`. array Logical indicating whether perform quantile normalization. Default `FALSE`. method Character string specifying method: `"DESeq2"` `"limma"`. Default `"DESeq2"`. contrast Character vector length 2 specifying contrast groups. Default `c("High", "Low")`. path Character string output directory. Default `NULL`. padj_cutoff Numeric cutoff adjusted p-values. Default `0.01`. logfc_cutoff Numeric log2 fold change cutoff. Default `0.5`. volcano_plot Logical indicating whether generate volcano plot. Default `FALSE`. col_volcano Integer specifying color index volcano plot. Default `1`. heatmap Logical indicating whether generate heatmap. Default `TRUE`. col_heatmap Integer specifying color index heatmap. Default `1`. parallel Logical indicating whether run parallel. Default `FALSE`.

title: Differential Expression Analysis — iobr_deg
text: Data frame containing differentially expressed genes statistics   including log2 fold changes adjusted p-values.

title: Differential Expression Analysis — iobr_deg
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Differential Expression Analysis — iobr_deg
code: # Simulate data set.seed(123) sim_eset <- matrix(abs(rnorm(100 * 20)), 100, 20) rownames(sim_eset) <- paste0("Gene", 1:100) colnames(sim_eset) <- paste0("Sample", 1:20)  sim_pdata <- data.frame(   ID = paste0("Sample", 1:20),   group = rep(c("High", "Low"), each = 10) )  # Run DEG analysis deg <- iobr_deg(   eset = sim_eset, pdata = sim_pdata,   group_id = "group", pdata_id = "ID",   method = "limma", contrast = c("High", "Low"),   heatmap = FALSE ) #> ℹ Matching grouping information and expression matrix #> ℹ Using limma for array differential analysis #> ℹ Group 1 = High #> ℹ Group 2 = Low if (!is.null(deg)) head(deg) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   symbol log2FoldChange AveExpr     t  pvalue  padj     B sigORnot label    High #>   <chr>           <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr>    <chr>   <dbl> #> 1 Gene29          0.813   0.808  3.27 0.00170 0.170 -1.25 NOT      log2FC… 1.21  #> 2 Gene16          0.697   1.00   2.56 0.0129  0.430 -2.94 NOT      log2FC… 1.35  #> 3 Gene96          0.686   0.732  2.57 0.0123  0.430 -2.90 NOT      log2FC… 1.07  #> 4 Gene43          0.605   0.903  2.23 0.0294  0.734 -3.60 NOT      log2FC… 1.21  #> 5 Gene28         -0.544   0.775 -2.10 0.0400  0.800 -3.84 NOT      log2FC… 0.503 #> 6 Gene12         -0.528   0.927 -1.89 0.0632  0.851 -4.20 NOT      log2FC… 0.663 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: Low <dbl>

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Visualization — iobr_pca
text: function performs Principal Component Analysis (PCA) gene expression data, reduces dimensionality preserving variance, generates scatter plot visualization.

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Visualization — iobr_pca
code: iobr_pca(   data,   is.matrix = TRUE,   scale = TRUE,   is.log = FALSE,   pdata,   id_pdata = "ID",   group = NULL,   geom.ind = "point",   cols = "normal",   palette = "jama",   repel = FALSE,   ncp = 5,   axes = c(1, 2),   addEllipses = TRUE )

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Visualization — iobr_pca
text: data Input data PCA: matrix data frame. .matrix Logical indicating input matrix. Default TRUE. scale Logical indicating whether scale data. Default TRUE. .log Logical indicating whether log-transform data. Default FALSE. pdata Data frame sample IDs grouping information. id_pdata Column name `pdata` sample IDs. Default "ID". group Column name `pdata` grouping variable. Default NULL. geom.ind Type geometric representation points. Default "point". cols Color scheme groups. Default "normal". palette Color palette groups. Default "jama". repel Logical indicating whether repel overlapping points. Default FALSE. ncp Number principal components retain. Default 5. axes Principal components plot (e.g., c(1, 2)). Default c(1, 2). addEllipses Logical indicating whether add concentration ellipses. Default TRUE.

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Visualization — iobr_pca
text: ggplot object PCA plot.

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Visualization — iobr_pca
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Visualization — iobr_pca
code: if (requireNamespace("FactoMineR", quietly = TRUE) &&   requireNamespace("factoextra", quietly = TRUE)) {   set.seed(123)   eset <- matrix(rnorm(1000), nrow = 100, ncol = 10)   rownames(eset) <- paste0("Gene", 1:100)   colnames(eset) <- paste0("Sample", 1:10)   pdata <- data.frame(     ID = colnames(eset),     group = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 5)   )   iobr_pca(eset, pdata = pdata, id_pdata = "ID", group = "group", addEllipses = FALSE) } #>  #> A B  #> 5 5  #> >>== colors for group:  #> >>== #374E55FF>>== #DF8F44FF #> Ignoring unknown labels: #> • fill : "group" #> • linetype : "group"

what: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
title: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
text: Applies LASSO (Least Absolute Shrinkage Selection Operator) regression construct predictive prognostic models. Supports binary survival response variables, utilizing cross-validation optimal model selection.

title: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
code: lasso_select(   x,   y,   type = c("binary", "survival"),   nfold = 10,   lambda = c("lambda.min", "lambda.1se") )

title: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
text: x numeric matrix. Features (e.g., gene symbols CGI) row names samples column names. y response variable vector. Can binary (0/1) survival data (e.g., survival time event status). type Character. Model type: "binary" binary response "survival" survival analysis. Default "binary". nfold Integer. Number folds cross-validation. Default 10. lambda Character. Regularization parameter selection: "lambda.min" (minimum mean cross-validated error) "lambda.1se" (one standard error minimum). Default "lambda.min".

title: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
text: Character vector selected feature names non-zero coefficients optimal LASSO model.

title: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Feature Selection for Predictive or Prognostic Models Using LASSO Regression — lasso_select
code: set.seed(123) gene_expression <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 20), nrow = 100, ncol = 20) rownames(gene_expression) <- paste0("Gene", 1:100) colnames(gene_expression) <- paste0("Sample", 1:20)  # Binary response example binary_outcome <- sample(c(0, 1), 20, replace = TRUE) lasso_select(   x = gene_expression,   y = binary_outcome,   type = "binary",   nfold = 5 ) #> Warning: one multinomial or binomial class has fewer than 8  observations; dangerous ground #> Warning: one multinomial or binomial class has fewer than 8  observations; dangerous ground #> Warning: one multinomial or binomial class has fewer than 8  observations; dangerous ground #> Warning: one multinomial or binomial class has fewer than 8  observations; dangerous ground #> Warning: one multinomial or binomial class has fewer than 8  observations; dangerous ground #> character(0)

what: Differential Expression Analysis Using Limma — limma.dif
title: Differential Expression Analysis Using Limma — limma.dif
text: Performs differential expression analysis using limma package given gene expression dataset. Constructs design matrix phenotype data, fits linear model, applies contrasts, computes statistics differential expression.

title: Differential Expression Analysis Using Limma — limma.dif
code: limma.dif(exprdata, pdata, contrastfml)

title: Differential Expression Analysis Using Limma — limma.dif
text: exprdata matrix rownames features like gene symbols cgi, colnames samples. pdata two-column dataframe first column matches colnames exprdata second column contains grouping variable. contrastfml character vector contrasts tested (see ?makeContrasts details).

title: Differential Expression Analysis Using Limma — limma.dif
text: Returns dataframe limma::topTable, includes genes rows columns like genelist, logFC, AveExpr, etc.

title: Differential Expression Analysis Using Limma — limma.dif
code: # Toy example with 100 genes and 6 samples set.seed(123) exprdata <- matrix(   rnorm(100 * 6),   nrow = 100,   ncol = 6,   dimnames = list(     paste0("gene", 1:100),     paste0("sample", 1:6)   ) )  # Phenotype data: 3 vs 3 pdata <- data.frame(   sample = colnames(exprdata),   group = rep(c("group1", "group2"), each = 3),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # Differential expression: group1 vs group2 res <- limma.dif(   exprdata = exprdata,   pdata = pdata,   contrastfml = "group1 - group2" ) head(res) #>            logFC     AveExpr         t    P.Value adj.P.Val         B #> gene16  2.146063  0.18155652  2.637222 0.01296770 0.6279722 -4.554407 #> gene91  1.979646 -0.19771191  2.533719 0.01657904 0.6279722 -4.557543 #> gene97  1.791447  0.65590789  2.412055 0.02199743 0.6279722 -4.561178 #> gene71 -1.738285  0.91534298 -2.330224 0.02650410 0.6279722 -4.563586 #> gene88  1.551421 -0.05545564  2.105013 0.04352443 0.6279722 -4.570024 #> gene56  1.644500 -0.43974674  2.038109 0.05018179 0.6279722 -4.571876

Available datasets include: - Expression data: `"eset_stad"`, `"imvigor210_eset"`, `"melanoma_data"` - Signatures: `"signature_tme"`, `"signature_metabolism"`, `"signature_collection"` - Gene sets: `"hallmark"`, `"kegg"`, `"go_bp"`, `"go_cc"`, `"go_mf"` - Cell markers: `"cellmarkers"`, `"mcp_genes"` - Phenotype data: `"pdata_stad"`, `"pdata_sig_tme"`, `"pdata_acrg"` - Reference data: `"xCell.data"`, `"quantiseq_data"`, `"TRef"`, `"BRef"` - Color palettes: `"palette1"`, `"palette2"`, `"palette3"`, `"palette4"`

what: Construct Mutation Matrices from MAF Data — make_mut_matrix
title: Construct Mutation Matrices from MAF Data — make_mut_matrix
text: Builds mutation presence/absence matrices MAF input (file path MAF object). Supports multiple categories: mutations, SNPs, indels, frameshift mutations. category = "multi", returns list matrices category. Compatible TCGA-formatted data.

title: Construct Mutation Matrices from MAF Data — make_mut_matrix
code: make_mut_matrix(   maf = NULL,   mut_data = NULL,   isTCGA = TRUE,   category = c("multi", "all", "snp", "indel", "frameshift"),   Tumor_Sample_Barcode = "Tumor_Sample_Barcode",   Hugo_Symbol = "Hugo_Symbol",   Variant_Classification = "Variant_Classification",   Variant_Type = "Variant_Type" )

title: Construct Mutation Matrices from MAF Data — make_mut_matrix
text: maf Character MAF object. Path MAF file already loaded MAF object. mut_data Data frame NULL. Preloaded MAF-like data (used 'maf' NULL). isTCGA Logical. Whether MAF follows TCGA conventions. Default TRUE. category Character. Mutation category: "", "snp", "indel", "frameshift", "multi". Default "multi". Tumor_Sample_Barcode Character. Column name tumor sample IDs. Default "Tumor_Sample_Barcode". Hugo_Symbol Character. Column name gene symbols. Default "Hugo_Symbol". Variant_Classification Character. Column name variant classification (e.g., Frame_Shift_Del). Default "Variant_Classification". Variant_Type Character. Column name variant type (e.g., SNP, INS, DEL). Default "Variant_Type".

what: Merge Data Frames with Duplicated Column Names — merge_duplicate
title: Merge Data Frames with Duplicated Column Names — merge_duplicate

title: Merge Data Frames with Duplicated Column Names — merge_duplicate
code: merge_duplicate(   x,   y,   by.x,   by.y,   all.x = FALSE,   all.y = FALSE,   all = NULL,   choose = c("x", "y") )

title: Merge Data Frames with Duplicated Column Names — merge_duplicate
text: x Data frame. First data frame merge. y Data frame. Second data frame merge. .x Character. Column name(s) `x` used merging. .y Character. Column name(s) `y` used merging. .x Logical. Include rows `x` output. Default `FALSE`. .y Logical. Include rows `y` output. Default `FALSE`. Logical `NULL`. `NULL`, include rows `x` `y`, overriding `.x` `.y`. choose Character. data frame's duplicated non-joining columns retain: `"x"` `"y"`. Default `"x"`.

title: Merge Data Frames with Duplicated Column Names — merge_duplicate
text: Data frame resulting merging `x` `y` according specified   parameters.

title: Merge Data Frames with Duplicated Column Names — merge_duplicate
code: df1 <- data.frame(ID = 1:3, Name = c("A", "B", "C"), Value = 1:3) df2 <- data.frame(ID = 1:3, Name = c("X", "Y", "Z"), Score = 4:6)  # Merge keeping duplicated columns from x merged_df <- merge_duplicate(df1, df2,   by.x = "ID", by.y = "ID",   all.x = TRUE, choose = "x" ) #> ℹ Removing 1 duplicate column from `y` print(merged_df) #>   ID Name Value Score #> 1  1    A     1     4 #> 2  2    B     2     5 #> 3  3    C     3     6  # Merge keeping duplicated columns from y merged_df2 <- merge_duplicate(df1, df2,   by.x = "ID", by.y = "ID",   all = TRUE, choose = "y" ) #> ℹ Removing 1 duplicate column from `x`

title: Merge Expression Sets by Row Names — merge_eset
text: eset1 First expression set (matrix data frame row names). eset2 Second expression set (matrix data frame row names). eset3 Optional third expression set. Default `NULL`.

title: Merge Expression Sets by Row Names — merge_eset
text: Merged expression set (data frame) duplicates removed.   Row names correspond gene symbols.

title: Merge Expression Sets by Row Names — merge_eset
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Merge Expression Sets by Row Names — merge_eset
code: # Create mock expression sets with common genes set.seed(123) common_genes <- c("TP53", "BRCA1", "EGFR", "MYC") eset1 <- matrix(rnorm(12),   nrow = 4,   dimnames = list(common_genes, paste0("S", 1:3)) ) eset2 <- matrix(rnorm(16),   nrow = 4,   dimnames = list(common_genes, paste0("S", 4:7)) ) # Merge two expression sets merged_eset <- merge_eset(eset1, eset2) #> ℹ Common genes between eset1 and eset2: 4 #> ℹ No duplicate gene symbols found. #> ✔ Final merged expression set: 4 genes x 7 samples print(dim(merged_eset)) #> [1] 4 7

what: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
title: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
text: Parallel version doPerm. Performs permutation-based sampling runs CoreAlg function iteratively using multiple CPU cores accelerate computation. function generates empirical null distribution correlation coefficients p-value calculation CIBERSORT analysis.

title: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
code: parallel_doperm(   perm1,   X1,   Y1,   absolute1,   abs_method1,   num_cores1 = 2,   seed = NULL )

title: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
text: perm1 Integer. Number permutations perform (\(\ge\)100 recommended). X1 Matrix data frame. Signature matrix (cell type GEP barcode). Y1 Matrix. Mixture file containing gene expression profiles. absolute1 Logical. Whether run absolute mode (default: FALSE). abs_method1 String. Method absolute mode: 'sig.score' '.sumto1'. num_cores1 Integer. Number CPU cores parallel computation (default: 2). seed Integer. Random seed reproducibility. NULL (default), uses current random state. Set specific value (e.g., 123) reproducible results across runs.

title: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
text: List containing: dist Numeric vector correlation coefficients permutations,     representing empirical null distribution.

title: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
text: function utilizes foreach doParallel packages distribute permutation iterations across multiple cores. automatically handles cluster setup/teardown via .exit() prevent resource leaks. Note: Windows users may experience slower performance due socket-based parallelization (PSOCK) versus forking Unix systems.

title: Parallel Permutation Test for CIBERSORT — parallel_doperm
code: # Simulate data set.seed(123) X <- matrix(rnorm(1000), nrow = 100) Y <- matrix(rnorm(500), nrow = 100) rownames(X) <- rownames(Y) <- paste0("Gene", 1:100) colnames(X) <- paste0("Cell", 1:10) colnames(Y) <- paste0("Sample", 1:5)  # Run parallel permutation test result <- parallel_doperm(   perm1 = 10, X1 = X, Y1 = Y,   absolute1 = FALSE, abs_method1 = "sig.score", num_cores1 = 1 ) #> Warning: already exporting variable(s): perm_indices, Y1, X1, absolute1, abs_method1 if (!is.null(result)) str(result$dist) #>  num [1:10, 1] 0.1653 0.0782 0.2331 0.3104 0.0609 ... #>  - attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>   ..$ : chr [1:10] "result.1" "result.2" "result.3" "result.4" ... #>   ..$ : NULL

Pathological stage (e.g. "Stage_I",   "Stage_II", "Stage_III", "Stage_IV"). status Factor. Vital status last follow-  ("Alive" "Dead"). Lauren Factor. EBV status tumour ("Positive"   "Negative"). time Numeric. Overall survival follow-time, typically   measured months. OS_status Integer/binary. Overall survival status indicator.   (1 = death, 0 = censored)

what: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
text: Removes batch effects expression datasets using sva::ComBat (microarray/TPM data) sva::ComBat_seq (RNA-seq count data). Generates PCA plots visualize data correction.

title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
code: remove_batcheffect(   eset1,   eset2,   eset3 = NULL,   id_type = "ensembl",   data_type = c("array", "count", "tpm"),   cols = "normal",   palette = "jama",   log2 = TRUE,   check_eset = TRUE,   adjust_eset = TRUE,   repel = FALSE,   path = NULL )

title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
text: eset1 First expression set (matrix data frame genes rows). eset2 Second expression set. eset3 Optional third expression set. Use `NULL` available. id_type Type gene ID expression sets (e.g., `"ensembl"`, `"symbol"`). Required count data normalization. data_type Type data: `"array"`, `"count"`, `"tpm"`. Default `"array"`. cols Color scale PCA plot. Default `"normal"`. palette Color palette PCA plot. Default `"jama"`. log2 Whether perform log2 transformation. Default `TRUE`. Ignored count data. check_eset Whether check expression sets errors. Default `TRUE`. adjust_eset Whether adjust expression sets removing problematic features. Default `TRUE`. repel Whether add repelling labels PCA plot. Default `FALSE`. path Directory results saved. Default `NULL` (display ).

title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
text: Expression matrix batch correction.

title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
text: Zhang Y, et al. ComBat-seq: batch effect adjustment RNA-seq count data. NAR Genomics Bioinformatics. 2020;2(3):lqaa078. doi:10.1093/nargab/lqaa078 Leek JT, et al. sva package removing batch effects unwanted variation high-throughput experiments. Bioinformatics. 2012;28(6):882-883.

title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Removing Batch Effect from Expression Sets — remove_batcheffect
code: # Simulate data set.seed(123) sim_eset1 <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 5, mean = 10, sd = 2), 100, 5) sim_eset2 <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 5, mean = 12, sd = 2), 100, 5) rownames(sim_eset1) <- rownames(sim_eset2) <- paste0("Gene", 1:100) colnames(sim_eset1) <- paste0("S1_", 1:5) colnames(sim_eset2) <- paste0("S2_", 1:5)  # Run batch correction if (requireNamespace("sva", quietly = TRUE) && requireNamespace("BiocParallel", quietly = TRUE)) {   eset_corrected <- remove_batcheffect(sim_eset1, sim_eset2, data_type = "tpm")   if (!is.null(eset_corrected)) head(eset_corrected) } #> ℹ Log2 transformation not necessary (data appears to already be log-scaled) #> ℹ Log2 transformation not necessary (data appears to already be log-scaled) #> ℹ The two expression matrices share 100 features #> ℹ Processing method: sva::ComBat #> Found2batches #> Adjusting for0covariate(s) or covariate level(s) #> Standardizing Data across genes #> Fitting L/S model and finding priors #> Finding parametric adjustments #> Adjusting the Data #> ℹ Log2 transformation not necessary (data appears to already be log-scaled) #>  #> eset1 eset2  #>     5     5  #> >>== colors for group:  #> >>== #374E55FF>>== #DF8F44FF #> ℹ Log2 transformation not necessary (data appears to already be log-scaled) #>  #> eset1 eset2  #>     5     5  #> >>== colors for group:  #> >>== #374E55FF>>== #DF8F44FF #>            S1_1      S1_2     S1_3      S1_4      S1_5      S2_1      S2_2 #> Gene1  9.792976  9.944992 14.91214  9.895600 10.695508 10.109164 13.361392 #> Gene2  9.976222 11.449648 12.48123  9.522742  8.426746  9.566679 11.449648 #> Gene3 14.048781 11.021545 10.59517  9.683681  9.629662 12.978768 10.960286 #> Gene4 11.418995 10.960286 12.22496  9.456422 10.960286 12.224961  8.749894 #> Gene5 11.275189  9.350557 10.10916 10.238317 12.145525  8.260313 12.317743 #> Gene6 13.906184 11.275189 10.00584 11.545079  8.123103 10.730401 10.831304 #>            S2_3     S2_4      S2_5 #> Gene1  9.566679 11.54508  9.236308 #> Gene2  8.577297 10.10916  9.976222 #> Gene3  9.456422 12.14552 11.578457 #> Gene4 10.653537 12.59930 13.558965 #> Gene5  8.849318 11.36695 12.820878 #> Gene6 12.145525 11.27519  9.944992

what: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
text: function addresses duplicate gene symbols gene expression dataset selecting highest-expressing instance among duplicates. Users can choose mean, standard deviation, sum ranking criterion selection. useful preparing data duplicates can lead issues downstream analyses.

title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
code: remove_duplicate_genes(eset, column_of_symbol, method = c("mean", "sd", "sum"))

title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
text: eset data frame matrix representing gene expression data, gene symbols one columns. column_of_symbol name column containing gene symbols `eset`. method ranking method use selecting among duplicate gene symbols: `"mean"` mean expression, `"sd"` standard deviation, `"sum"` sum expression values. Default `"mean"`.

title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
text: modified version `eset` duplicate gene symbols   reduced single entry (highest-ranking one). gene symbols   set row names returned data frame.

title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
text: Important: function performs selection, aggregation. duplicate genes, retains highest-ranking instance (based specified method) discards others.

title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Remove Duplicate Gene Symbols in Gene Expression Data — remove_duplicate_genes
code: set.seed(123) test_eset <- data.frame(   symbol = c("GeneA", "GeneA", "GeneB", "GeneC"),   S1 = c(10, 5, 20, 15),   S2 = c(12, 7, 22, 17) ) # Remove duplicates using mean expression test_eset_unique <- remove_duplicate_genes(   eset = test_eset,   column_of_symbol = "symbol",   method = "mean" ) #> ℹ Found 1 duplicate symbol. Using "mean" for ranking. #> ✔ Reduced to 3 unique genes print(test_eset_unique) #>       S1 S2 #> GeneB 20 22 #> GeneC 15 17 #> GeneA 10 12

text: Dorothee Nickles, Dongqiang Zeng

title: Calculate Signature Score Using PCA, Mean, or Z-score Methods — sigScore
code: # Create small example expression matrix set.seed(123) test_eset <- matrix(rnorm(1000), nrow = 10, ncol = 100) rownames(test_eset) <- paste0("Gene", 1:10) colnames(test_eset) <- paste0("Sample", 1:100)  # Calculate scores score_pca <- sigScore(eset = test_eset, methods = "PCA") score_mean <- sigScore(eset = test_eset, methods = "mean") score_zscore <- sigScore(eset = test_eset, methods = "zscore")

Default `"nrc"`. cols Character vector custom fill colors. `NULL`, palette used. Default `NULL`. jitter Logical indicating whether add jittered points box plot. Default `FALSE`. point_size Numeric value specifying size jittered points. Default 5. angle_x_text Numeric value specifying rotation angle x-axis labels (degrees). Default 0. hjust Numeric value specifying horizontal justification x-axis labels. Default 0.5. show_pairwise_p Logical indicating whether display pairwise comparison p-values groups. Default `TRUE`. show_overall_p Logical indicating whether display overall group difference p-value. Default `FALSE`. return_stat_res Logical indicating whether return statistical test results instead plot. Default `FALSE`. size_of_pvalue Numeric value specifying font size p-values. Default 6. size_of_font Numeric value specifying base font size. Default 10. assay Character string specifying assay name (Seurat objects). Default `NULL`. slot Character string specifying slot name (Seurat objects). Default `"scale.data"`. scale Logical indicating whether scale signature values (z-score transformation). Default `FALSE`.

text: input Data frame Seurat object containing data analysis. vars Character vector. Features variables analyze. `pattern_vars = TRUE`, treated regular expression patterns. groups Character vector. Grouping variable(s) comparison. pattern_vars Logical indicating whether treat `vars` regular expression patterns matching column names. Default `FALSE`. path Character string `NULL`. Directory save plots. `NULL`, plots saved. Default `NULL`. index Integer. Starting index plot filenames. Default `0`. angle_x_text Numeric. Angle x-axis labels degrees. Default `0`. hjust Numeric. Horizontal justification x-axis labels. Default `0.5`. palette Character. Color palette plots. Default `"jama"`. cols Character vector `NULL`. Default `TRUE`. return_stat_res Logical indicating whether return statistical results instead saving plots. Default `FALSE`. assay Character string `NULL`. Assay type Seurat objects. slot Character. Data slot Seurat objects. Default `"scale.data"`. scale Logical indicating whether scale data analysis. Default `FALSE`. height Numeric. Height plots inches. Default `5`. width Numeric. Width plots inches. Default `3.5`. fig_type Character. File format plots (e.g., `"pdf"`, `"png"`). Default `"pdf"`. max_count_feas Integer. Maximum number features analyze `pattern_vars = TRUE`. matched variables exceed limit, first `max_count_feas` features used. Default `30`.

what: Perform Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — sig_gsea
title: Perform Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — sig_gsea
text: Conducts Gene Set Enrichment Analysis identify significantly enriched gene sets differential gene expression data. Supports MSigDB gene sets custom gene signatures, generates comprehensive visualizations statistical results.

title: Perform Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — sig_gsea
code: sig_gsea(   deg,   genesets = NULL,   path = NULL,   gene_symbol = "symbol",   logfc = "log2FoldChange",   org = c("hsa", "mus"),   msigdb = TRUE,   category = "H",   subcategory = NULL,   palette_bar = "jama",   palette_gsea = 2,   cols_gsea = NULL,   cols_bar = NULL,   show_bar = 10,   show_col = FALSE,   show_plot = FALSE,   show_gsea = 8,   show_path_n = 20,   plot_single_sig = FALSE,   project = "custom_sig",   minGSSize = 10,   maxGSSize = 500,   verbose = TRUE,   seed = FALSE,   fig.type = "pdf",   print_bar = TRUE )

title: Perform Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — sig_gsea
text: deg Data frame containing differential expression results gene symbols log fold changes. genesets List custom gene sets enrichment analysis. `NULL`, MSigDB gene sets used based `org` `category`. Default `NULL`. path Character string specifying directory path saving results. Default `NULL`. gene_symbol Character string specifying column name `deg` containing gene symbols. Default `"symbol"`. logfc Character string specifying column name `deg` containing log fold change values. Default `"log2FoldChange"`. org Character string specifying organism. Options `"hsa"` (Homo sapiens) `"mus"` (Mus musculus). Default `"hsa"`. msigdb Logical indicating whether use MSigDB gene sets. Default `TRUE`. category Character string specifying MSigDB category (e.g., `"H"` Hallmark, `"C2"` curated gene sets). Default `"H"`. subcategory Character string specifying MSigDB subcategory filter gene sets. Default `NULL`. palette_bar Character string integer specifying color palette bar plots. Default `"jama"`. palette_gsea Integer specifying color palette GSEA plots. Default `2`. cols_gsea Character vector specifying custom colors GSEA enrichment plots. `NULL`, colors automatically generated. Default `NULL`. cols_bar Character vector specifying custom colors enrichment bar plot. `NULL`, colors automatically generated. Default `NULL`. show_bar Integer specifying number top enriched gene sets display bar plot. Default `10`. show_col Logical indicating whether display color names bar plot. Default `FALSE`. show_plot Logical indicating whether display GSEA enrichment plots. Default `FALSE`. show_gsea Integer specifying number top significant gene sets generate GSEA plots. Default `8`. show_path_n Integer specifying number pathways display GSEA plots. Default `20`. plot_single_sig Logical indicating whether generate separate plots significant gene set. Default `TRUE`. project Character string specifying project name output files. Default `"custom_sig"`. minGSSize Integer specifying minimum gene set size analysis. Default `10`. maxGSSize Integer specifying maximum gene set size analysis. Default `500`. verbose Logical indicating whether display progress messages. Default `TRUE`. seed Logical indicating whether set random seed reproducibility. Default `FALSE`. fig.type Character string specifying file format saving plots (e.g., `"pdf"`, `"png"`). Default `"pdf"`. print_bar Logical indicating whether save print bar plot. Default `TRUE`.

title: Perform Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — sig_gsea
text: List containing: Data frame -regulated enriched gene sets Data frame -regulated enriched gene sets Complete GSEA results plot_top GSEA enrichment plot top gene sets

title: Perform Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — sig_gsea
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Perform Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) — sig_gsea
code: set.seed(123) genes <- c(   "TP53", "BRCA1", "EGFR", "MYC", "KRAS", "PTEN", "APC", "RB1",   "CDKN2A", "VHL", "ATM", "ATR", "CHEK2", "PALB2", "RAD51", "MDM2",   "CDK4", "CDK6", "CCND1", "CCNE1", "CDK2", "E2F1", "E2F2", "E2F3",   "ARF1", "ARF3", "ARF4", "ARF5", "ARF6", "GSK3B", "AKT1", "AKT2",   "PIK3CA", "PIK3CB", "PIK3CD", "PIK3CG", "PIK3R1", "PIK3R2", "PIK3R3" ) deg <- data.frame(   symbol = genes,   log2FoldChange = rnorm(length(genes), mean = 0, sd = 2),   padj = runif(length(genes), 0, 0.1) ) signature <- list(   DNA_Repair = c(     "TP53", "BRCA1", "ATM",     "ATR", "CHEK2", "PALB2", "RAD51"   ),   Cell_Cycle = c(     "TP53", "MYC",     "RB1", "CDKN2A", "CDK4",     "CDK6", "CCND1", "CCNE1",     "CDK2", "E2F1", "E2F2", "E2F3"   ),   PI3K_AKT = c(     "AKT1", "AKT2",     "PIK3CA", "PIK3CB", "PIK3CD",     "PIK3CG", "PIK3R1", "PIK3R2", "PIK3R3"   ) ) # \donttest{ res <- sig_gsea(   deg = deg,   genesets = signature,   path = tempdir(),   show_plot = FALSE,   print_bar = FALSE ) #> ℹ Mapping gene symbols to Entrez IDs for "hsa" #>  #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> Warning: number of columns of result is not a multiple of vector length (arg 1) #> ✔ Signature data saved to /tmp/Rtmp6CKTc5/file1d0c59e04478.csv #> 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns #> Warning: 3.57% of input gene IDs are fail to map... #> ℹ Running GSEA analysis... #> ✔ GSEA results written to: /tmp/Rtmp6CKTc5/1-H_GSEA_significant_results.csv #> ℹ Most significant gene sets: Cell_Cycle print(names(res)) #> [1] "up"       "down"     "all"      "plot_top" # }

what: Subgroup Survival Analysis Using Cox Proportional Hazards Models — subgroup_survival

text: Extracts hazard ratios (HR) 95 proportional hazards models across specified subgroups.

code: subgroup_survival(   pdata,   time_name = "time",   status_name = "status",   variables,   object )

title: Subgroup Survival Analysis Using Cox Proportional Hazards Models — subgroup_survival
text: pdata Data frame containing variables, follow-time, outcome. time_name Character. Column name follow-time. Default `"time"`. status_name Character. Column name event status (1/0). Default `"status"`. variables Character vector. Subgrouping variables (processed independently). object Character. Variable interest used Cox model. levels "High" "Low", recode "High" 0 "Low" 1 calling.

title: Subgroup Survival Analysis Using Cox Proportional Hazards Models — subgroup_survival
text: Data frame summarizing subgroup Cox results (HR, CI, p-value).

title: Subgroup Survival Analysis Using Cox Proportional Hazards Models — subgroup_survival
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Subgroup Survival Analysis Using Cox Proportional Hazards Models — subgroup_survival
code: set.seed(123) test_pdata <- data.frame(   time = runif(100, 1, 100),   status = sample(c(0, 1), 100, replace = TRUE),   subgroup = sample(c("A", "B"), 100, replace = TRUE),   score = rnorm(100) ) res <- subgroup_survival(   pdata = test_pdata,   time_name = "time",   status_name = "status",   variables = "subgroup",   object = "score" ) print(res) #>                    P     HR CI_low_0.95 CI_up_0.95 #> subgroup_A 0.8258723 1.0460      0.7009     1.5608 #> subgroup_B 0.5137623 0.8618      0.5515     1.3467

what: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plots for Categorical Groups — surv_group
title: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plots for Categorical Groups — surv_group
text: Creates Kaplan-Meier survival plots data grouped categorical variable. Handles binary multi-level categorical groups customizable plot aesthetics.

title: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plots for Categorical Groups — surv_group
code: surv_group(   input_pdata,   target_group,   ID = "ID",   levels = c("High", "Low"),   reference_group = NULL,   project = NULL,   time = "time",   status = "status",   time_type = "month",   break_month = "auto",   cols = NULL,   palette = "jama",   mini_sig = "score",   save_path = NULL,   fig.type = "pdf",   index = 1,   width = 6,   height = 6.5,   font.size.table = 3 )

title: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plots for Categorical Groups — surv_group
text: input_pdata Data frame containing survival data grouping variables. target_group Name column containing grouping variable. ID Name column unique identifiers. Default "ID". levels Names levels target_group (binary groups). Default c("High", "Low"). reference_group Reference level binary comparison. Default NULL. project Optional title plot. Default NULL. time Name column follow-times. Default "time". status Name column event indicators. Default "status". time_type Units: "month" "day". Default "month". break_month X-axis break interval. "auto", calculated automatically. Default "auto". cols Color vector plot lines. Default NULL. palette Color palette name. Default "jama". mini_sig Prefix label variables. Default "score". save_path Directory saving plot. Default NULL. fig.type File format: "pdf" "png". Default "pdf". index Identifier file naming. Default 1. width Plot width. Default 6. height Plot height. Default 6.5. font.size.table Font size risk table. Default 3.

title: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plots for Categorical Groups — surv_group
text: Kaplan-Meier plot object.

title: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plots for Categorical Groups — surv_group
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Generate Kaplan-Meier Survival Plots for Categorical Groups — surv_group
code: # Simulate data set.seed(123) sim_pdata <- data.frame(   ID = paste0("Sample", 1:100),   Lauren = sample(c("Intestinal", "Diffuse"), 100, replace = TRUE),   time = runif(100, 1, 60),   OS_status = sample(0:1, 100, replace = TRUE) ) # Run survival analysis (survival and survminer are imported packages) result <- surv_group(   input_pdata = sim_pdata,   target_group = "Lauren",   time = "time",   status = "OS_status",   save_path = NULL ) #> ℹ Follow-up time ranges from 1.62 to 59.15 months #>    Diffuse Intestinal  #>         43         57  #> ℹ Maximum follow-up time is 59.2 months; divided into 6 sections #> ℹ Reference group not defined, using alphabetical order #> Ignoring unknown labels: #> • colour : "Strata" if (!is.null(result)) print(result)  #> [[1]] #> NULL #>

title: Preprocess TCGA RNA-seq Data — tcga_rna_preps
text: Preprocesses TCGA RNA-seq data modifying sample types, transforming data, annotating genes based specified parameters.

title: Preprocess TCGA RNA-seq Data — tcga_rna_preps
code: tcga_rna_preps(   eset,   id_type = c("ensembl", "symbol"),   input_type = c("log2count", "count"),   output = c("tumor", "tumor_normal"),   output_type = c("tpm", "log2tpm", "count"),   annotation = TRUE )

title: Preprocess TCGA RNA-seq Data — tcga_rna_preps
text: eset Matrix data frame. RNA-seq gene expression matrix TCGA. id_type Character. Gene identifier type: "ensembl" "symbol". Default "ensembl". input_type Character. Input data type: "log2count" "count". Default "log2count". output Character. Sample type: "tumor" "tumor_normal". Default "tumor". output_type Character. Output data type: "tpm", "log2tpm", "count". Default "tpm". annotation Logical. Whether perform gene annotation. Default TRUE.

title: Preprocess TCGA RNA-seq Data — tcga_rna_preps
text: Preprocessed gene expression matrix.

title: Preprocess TCGA RNA-seq Data — tcga_rna_preps
text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Preprocess TCGA RNA-seq Data — tcga_rna_preps
code: # Simulate TCGA-style data with Ensembl IDs set.seed(123) sim_eset <- matrix(   abs(rnorm(500)),   nrow = 100,   ncol = 5,   dimnames = list(     paste0("ENSG00000", 101:200),     paste0("TCGA-AB-123", 4:8, "-01A")   ) ) # Process tumor samples only (output as count for offline testing) eset <- tcga_rna_preps(   eset = sim_eset, id_type = "ensembl", input_type = "count",   output = "tumor", output_type = "count", annotation = FALSE ) #> ℹ Sample type distribution: #>  #> 01A  #>   5  #> ℹ Adjacent normal samples removed #> ℹ TCGA barcode reduced to 12 digits #> ℹ Duplicate barcode check: #>    Mode   FALSE  #> logical       5  print(dim(eset)) #> [1] 100   5

title: Identification of TME Cluster — tme_cluster
text: Performs TME (Tumor Microenvironment) clustering analysis using various clustering methods. Supports feature selection, scaling, automatic determination optimal cluster number.

title: Identification of TME Cluster — tme_cluster
text: input Data frame containing input dataset. features Vector features use clustering. Default NULL (uses columns pattern-selected columns). pattern Regular expression pattern selecting features. Default NULL. id Column name identifiers. Default NULL (uses row names). scale Logical indicating whether scale features. Default TRUE. method Clustering method. Default "kmeans". min_nc Minimum number clusters evaluate. Default 2. max.nc Maximum number clusters evaluate. Default 6.

text: Data frame cluster assignments appended.

text: Dongqiang Zeng

title: Identification of TME Cluster — tme_cluster
code: set.seed(123) input_data <- data.frame(   ID = paste0("Sample", 1:20),   xCell_Tcells = rnorm(20),   xCell_Bcells = rnorm(20),   xCell_Macrophages = rnorm(20),   Other_feature = rnorm(20) ) result <- tme_cluster(   input = input_data,   pattern = "xCell",   id = "ID",   method = "kmeans" ) #> ℹ Best number of TME clusters: 3 #> ℹ Cluster distribution: #>  1  2  3  #> 10  8  2  table(result$cluster) #>  #> TME1 TME2 TME3  #>   10    8    2

title: Transform NA, Inf, or Zero Values in Data — transform_data
code: data_matrix <- data.frame(   A = c(1, 2, NA, 4, Inf),   B = c(Inf, 2, 3, 4, 5),   C = c(0, 0, 0, 1, 2) )  # Replace NAs with 0 transform_data(data_matrix, feature = c("A", "B"), data_type = "NA") #>     A   B C #> 1   1 Inf 0 #> 2   2   2 0 #> 3   0   3 0 #> 4   4   4 1 #> 5 Inf   5 2  # Replace Inf values with the mean of the column transform_data(data_matrix,   feature = c("A", "B"),   data_type = "Inf", into = "mean" ) #>          A   B C #> 1 1.000000 3.5 0 #> 2 2.000000 2.0 0 #> 3       NA 3.0 0 #> 4 4.000000 4.0 1 #> 5 2.333333 5.0 2  # Replace zeros with -1 in column C transform_data(data_matrix, feature = "C", data_type = "zero", into = -1) #>     A   B  C #> 1   1 Inf -1 #> 2   2   2 -1 #> 3  NA   3 -1 #> 4   4   4  1 #> 5 Inf   5  2

title: The xCell Analysis Pipeline — xCellAnalysis
text: expr Gene expression data matrix row names gene symbols columns samples. signatures GMT object signatures. `NULL`, uses xCell defaults. genes Character vector genes use analysis. `NULL`, uses xCell defaults. spill Spillover object adjusting scores. `NULL`, uses xCell defaults. rnaseq Logical indicating whether use RNA-seq (TRUE) array (FALSE) spillover parameters. Default `TRUE`. file.name Character string saving scores. Default `NULL`. scale Logical indicating whether use scaling. Default `TRUE`. alpha Numeric value override spillover alpha parameter. Default `0.5`. save.raw Logical indicating whether save raw scores. Default `FALSE`. cell.types.use Character vector cell types use. `NULL`, uses available cell types. Default `NULL`.

text: Calculates FDR-adjusted p-values using random shuffled matrix.

title: Calculate Significance Using Random Matrix — xCellSignifcanceRandomMatrix
code: xCellSignifcanceRandomMatrix(   scores,   expr,   spill,   alpha = 0.5,   nperm = 250,   file.name = NULL )

title: Calculate Significance Using Random Matrix — xCellSignifcanceRandomMatrix
text: scores xCell scores matrix. expr Input expression matrix. spill Spillover object. alpha Spillover alpha parameter. Default `0.5`. nperm Number permutations. Default `250`. file.name Character string saving p-values. Default `NULL`.

title: Calculate Significance Using Random Matrix — xCellSignifcanceRandomMatrix
text: List containing p-values, shuffled xCell scores, shuffled expression,   beta distributions.

title: IOBR 2.2.3
text: Default cache directory changed tools::R_user_dir() session-specific temporary directory. Added internet connectivity check Google Baidu (Chinese users). Fixed example handling: replaced (interactive()) proper \dontrun{} blocks genuinely non-runable examples (network-dependent, user files required, Suggested packages). CRAN clarified (interactive()) skip examples checks. Minimized \dontrun{} usage 22 blocks documented reasons; functions can run offline use simulated data. Users can still opt-persistent caching via set_iobr_cache_dir() options(IOBR.cache_dir = ...).

title: IOBR 2.2.3
text: Fixed count2tpm() return NULL gracefully annotation data unavailable offline.

title: IOBR 2.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-22

title: IOBR 2.2.0
text: Fixed functions wrote user’s home filespace (working directory) default. Writing now occurs explicit path / save_path / output.dir provided. Affected functions: eset_distribution(), find_outlier_samples(), iobr_cor_plot(), sig_pheatmap(), sig_box_batch(), plotPurity(), IPS_calculation(), find_mutations(), sig_gsea(), get_cor(), batch_sig_surv_plot(), format_signatures(), creat_folder(). Examples updated use tempdir() file writing demonstrated.

title: IOBR 2.2.0
text: find_mutations(): Fixed semantic naming error file_name variable used store directory paths. Renamed output_dir clarity. Fixed ggsave() parameter order issues. iobr_cor_plot(): Fixed ggsave() parameter order issues. correct order filename first, plot. surv_group(): Fixed ggsave() parameter order issues. roc_time(): Fixed ggsave() parameter order issues. batch_sig_surv_plot(): Changed default save_path file.path(tempdir(), "Multiple-KM-plot") NULL prevent automatic directory creation. format_signatures(): Changed parameter name output_name output_path consistency. Added validation requiring output_path save_signature = TRUE. find_outlier_samples(): Added validation requiring project save = TRUE. plotPurity(): Changed default output.dir "estimated_purity_plots" NULL.

title: IOBR 2.2.0
text: CRAN Size Compliance: Moved large datasets (>5MB total) R/sysdata.rda data/ GitHub Releases meet CRAN package size requirements. Data now downloaded -demand cached locally. Added load_data() function unified data access (supports sysdata, exported data, GitHub-hosted datasets) Added download_iobr_data() multiple mirror support (GitHub, ghproxy.vip, gh-proxy.org, ghfast.top) Added add_iobr_mirror() custom mirror configuration Added list_github_datasets() show available remote datasets Added clear_iobr_cache() manage downloaded data Automatic Caching: Downloaded datasets cached user’s cache directory persist across package updates. Fixed count2tpm() handle symbol-based gene IDs robustly.

title: IOBR 2.2.0
text: following datasets now hosted GitHub downloaded first use: - Reference matrices: BRef, TRef, lm22 - Annotations: anno_gc_vm32, anno_grch38, anno_hug133plus2, anno_illumina, anno_rnaseq - Example datasets: tcga_stad_sig, imvigor210_sig, eset_stad, sig_stad, eset_gse62254, etc. - Gene sets: hallmark, kegg, go_bp, go_cc, go_mf, reactome, msig_immune, msig_sc - Cell markers: cancer_type_genes, cellmarkers, common_genes, immuneCuratedData, ips_gene_set, SI_geneset, mRNA_cell_default, mus_human_gene_symbol, onco_sig, PurityDataAffy - Reference data: xCell.data, quantiseq_data - Signatures: signature_collection_citation, signature_metabolism, signature_sc, signature_tumor

title: IOBR 2.2.0
text: Updated internal code use load_data() instead direct object references migrated datasets Updated globalVariables.R reflect data migration Enhanced error messages manual download instructions mirrors fail

title: IOBR 2.1.1
text: Added Docker support automated image builds publishing GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io/iobr/iobr). image based rocker/tidyverse includes IOBR dependencies pre-installed (#Docker). Added Conda/Mamba installation instructions README easier environment setup. Expanded iobr_cor_plot() support multi-group (3+) comparisons using Kruskal-Wallis test, addition existing two-group Wilcoxon test (#28).

title: IOBR 2.1.1
text: Fixed generateRef_DEseq2() handle sparse single-cell RNA-seq data using type = "poscounts" size factor estimation, preventing errors genes contain least one zero. Fixed batch_kruskal() output format correctly display mean-centered values groups. Fixed count2tpm() computation results align previous correct versions. Updated count2tpm() documentation clarify gene identifiers converted gene symbols output regardless input ID type. Wrapped long-running examples \dontrun{} \donttest{} comply CRAN check time limits.

title: IOBR 2.1.1
text: Refactored internal helper functions count2tpm.R better code organization maintainability. Updated load_data() documentation include additional available datasets.

title: IOBR 2.1.0
text: Initial CRAN submission.
